messages.content: You produce rigorous invention-deduction traces: constraint-grounded, failure-aware, and testable. Separate deductions from speculative abductive leaps.

Convert the supplied project evidence into a structured invention-deduction trace. Preserve constraints, separate deductions from abductive leaps, include failure modes, and propose a minimal falsification test.

Input:
{
  "task_type": "failed_invention_diagnosis",
  "domain": "neural memory mechanisms",
  "problem": "A memory routing experiment collapsed to encoder-skip/no-write behavior with near-zero task accuracy.",
  "objective": "Diagnose why the mechanism failed and derive a repairable training curriculum.",
  "constraints": [
    "must distinguish feasibility from optimization failure",
    "track write/read/skip rates",
    "use oracle sanity check",
    "avoid empty-read behavior"
  ],
  "prior_art_or_comparators": [
    "external memory networks",
    "attention routers",
    "DAGGER/imitation",
    "oracle policies"
  ]
}

{
  "id": "zach_failed_invention_diagnosis_0001_attention-memory-router-collapse",
  "title": "Attention Memory Router Collapse",
  "trace_type": "failed_invention_diagnosis",
  "domain": "neural memory mechanisms",
  "problem": "A memory routing experiment collapsed to encoder-skip/no-write behavior with near-zero task accuracy.",
  "objective": "Diagnose why the mechanism failed and derive a repairable training curriculum.",
  "constraints": [
    "must distinguish feasibility from optimization failure",
    "track write/read/skip rates",
    "use oracle sanity check",
    "avoid empty-read behavior"
  ],
  "prior_art": [
    "external memory networks",
    "attention routers",
    "DAGGER/imitation",
    "oracle policies"
  ],
  "observations": [
    "B=10 run showed skip near 0.94, write_encoder 0.0, query_read 0.5 and accuracy around 0.02.",
    "B=50 or oracle settings demonstrated feasible high accuracy."
  ],
  "deductions": [
    "If oracle solves the task, the task is feasible.",
    "If learned router never writes but sometimes reads, it is reading unhelpful memory.",
    "Therefore the failure is a learned-policy collapse, not a missing-information impossibility."
  ],
  "abductive_leaps": [
    "Use oracle traces as an imitation curriculum before free routing."
  ],
  "candidate_mechanism": "Anti-Collapse Memory Routing Curriculum",
  "mechanism_steps": [
    "collect oracle write/read traces",
    "pretrain router on phase behavior",
    "penalize empty reads and no-write collapse",
    "anneal imitation loss",
    "stress test low memory budgets"
  ],
  "predicted_benefits": [
    "stabilized memory use",
    "clearer diagnosis",
    "reusable negative traces"
  ],
  "failure_modes": [
    "regularization forces useless writes",
    "oracle imitation blocks better policy",
    "synthetic transfer weak"
  ],
  "minimal_test": "Compare free router, oracle imitation and anti-collapse penalties across B=10/B=50 and seeds.",
  "evaluation_metrics": [
    "accuracy",
    "write rate",
    "read rate",
    "empty-read rate",
    "skip rate",
    "seed stability"
  ],
  "source_basis": [
    "personal_context: attention repo diag_dagger latest B=10 fail, earlier B=50 and oracle sanity results."
  ],
  "provenance": {
    "origin": "synthesized_from_user_uploaded_archives_and_project_chat_history",
    "uploaded_files_used": [
      "TGChat export.zip",
      "fine_tune_dataset.zip"
    ],
    "redaction_applied": true,
    "raw_private_messages_included": false,
    "curation_note": "Gold-candidate record synthesized from project-specific traces, summaries, and notes; should be human-reviewed before public release."
  },
  "labels": {
    "deduction_quality": 0.91,
    "novelty": 0.64,
    "plausibility": 0.88,
    "testability": 0.97,
    "implementation_difficulty": 0.68
  },
  "tags": [
    "failure",
    "memory",
    "router"
  ]
}

Input:
{
  "task_type": "failed_invention_diagnosis",
  "domain": "LLM inference memory",
  "problem": "An ambitious unified-memory KV tier design risks decode-time stalls from bursty page faults.",
  "objective": "Diagnose why naive unified memory undermines the latency objective.",
  "constraints": [
    "decode path latency sensitive",
    "no NVMe in decode",
    "fault budget required",
    "tiers must be few"
  ],
  "prior_art_or_comparators": [
    "CUDA unified memory",
    "KV offload",
    "paged attention",
    "QFUME F0-F4 tiers"
  ]
}

{
  "id": "zach_failed_invention_diagnosis_0002_unified-memory-page-fault-storm-in-qfume",
  "title": "Unified Memory Page-Fault Storm in QFUME",
  "trace_type": "failed_invention_diagnosis",
  "domain": "LLM inference memory",
  "problem": "An ambitious unified-memory KV tier design risks decode-time stalls from bursty page faults.",
  "objective": "Diagnose why naive unified memory undermines the latency objective.",
  "constraints": [
    "decode path latency sensitive",
    "no NVMe in decode",
    "fault budget required",
    "tiers must be few"
  ],
  "prior_art": [
    "CUDA unified memory",
    "KV offload",
    "paged attention",
    "QFUME F0-F4 tiers"
  ],
  "observations": [
    "Assistant cross-examined QFUME and warned UM is bursty/page-fault prone.",
    "Revision reduced to H0/H1/H2 with proactive fault budgeting."
  ],
  "deductions": [
    "If page faults occur reactively during decode, latency spikes can exceed memory savings.",
    "If the system has too many tiers, controller tuning becomes fragile.",
    "Therefore QFUME must bound fault behavior before optimizing fidelity."
  ],
  "abductive_leaps": [
    "Replace broad UM reliance with proactive fault budget and monotonic tier stack."
  ],
  "candidate_mechanism": "QFUME-R Failure Repair",
  "mechanism_steps": [
    "instrument page faults/token",
    "demote proactively before pressure event",
    "cap migrations per decode window",
    "fallback to high-fidelity retention under uncertainty"
  ],
  "predicted_benefits": [
    "latency-safe memory savings",
    "fewer tail spikes"
  ],
  "failure_modes": [
    "overconservative fallback loses savings",
    "fault predictor misses bursts"
  ],
  "minimal_test": "Inject memory pressure and compare reactive UM tiering to proactive QFUME-R on P99 and faults/token.",
  "evaluation_metrics": [
    "faults/token",
    "P99 latency",
    "memory saved",
    "fallback rate"
  ],
  "source_basis": [
    "personal_context: QFUME warning and QFUME-R revision."
  ],
  "provenance": {
    "origin": "synthesized_from_user_uploaded_archives_and_project_chat_history",
    "uploaded_files_used": [
      "TGChat export.zip",
      "fine_tune_dataset.zip"
    ],
    "redaction_applied": true,
    "raw_private_messages_included": false,
    "curation_note": "Gold-candidate record synthesized from project-specific traces, summaries, and notes; should be human-reviewed before public release."
  },
  "labels": {
    "deduction_quality": 0.88,
    "novelty": 0.78,
    "plausibility": 0.78,
    "testability": 0.94,
    "implementation_difficulty": 0.68
  },
  "tags": [
    "failure",
    "kv",
    "unified-memory"
  ]
}

Input:
{
  "task_type": "failed_invention_diagnosis",
  "domain": "symbolic sequence modeling",
  "problem": "A local PPM/context predictor improves shallow tasks but degrades when nested dependencies require deeper state.",
  "objective": "Diagnose why bounded local memory is insufficient for code-like language.",
  "constraints": [
    "integer-only constraint",
    "must support nesting",
    "must measure state-depth effects",
    "avoid simply increasing state_bits blindly"
  ],
  "prior_art_or_comparators": [
    "PPM",
    "FSM",
    "PDA",
    "CP-SAT",
    "context-tree models"
  ]
}

{
  "id": "zach_failed_invention_diagnosis_0003_circuit-lm-local-predictor-degrades-with-depth",
  "title": "Circuit-LM Local Predictor Degrades with Depth",
  "trace_type": "failed_invention_diagnosis",
  "domain": "symbolic sequence modeling",
  "problem": "A local PPM/context predictor improves shallow tasks but degrades when nested dependencies require deeper state.",
  "objective": "Diagnose why bounded local memory is insufficient for code-like language.",
  "constraints": [
    "integer-only constraint",
    "must support nesting",
    "must measure state-depth effects",
    "avoid simply increasing state_bits blindly"
  ],
  "prior_art": [
    "PPM",
    "FSM",
    "PDA",
    "CP-SAT",
    "context-tree models"
  ],
  "observations": [
    "Project memory notes PDA-2ph improved with depth while PPM degraded; assistant noted FSM-only bounded memory cap."
  ],
  "deductions": [
    "If dependencies cross nested scopes, local n-gram/context trees miss stack state.",
    "If adding finite states is the only fix, state explosion follows.",
    "Therefore the model needs explicit stack/counter augmentation."
  ],
  "abductive_leaps": [
    "Promote stack state to first-class predictor feature."
  ],
  "candidate_mechanism": "PDA-Augmented Circuit-LM Repair",
  "mechanism_steps": [
    "add stack_top and stack depth to state",
    "separate push/pop configs",
    "train on nested synthetic tasks",
    "compare against PPM at increasing depth"
  ],
  "predicted_benefits": [
    "better depth scaling",
    "interpretable failures",
    "integer-only path preserved"
  ],
  "failure_modes": [
    "stack features overfit grammar",
    "more states slow decoding"
  ],
  "minimal_test": "Sweep nesting depth and compare PPM, FSM, PDA-2ph and joint-PDA small models.",
  "evaluation_metrics": [
    "accuracy by depth",
    "state count",
    "runtime",
    "valid syntax rate"
  ],
  "source_basis": [
    "personal_context: Circuit-LM results PDA improved with depth, PPM degraded; push/pop configs and joint-PDA tests."
  ],
  "provenance": {
    "origin": "synthesized_from_user_uploaded_archives_and_project_chat_history",
    "uploaded_files_used": [
      "TGChat export.zip",
      "fine_tune_dataset.zip"
    ],
    "redaction_applied": true,
    "raw_private_messages_included": false,
    "curation_note": "Gold-candidate record synthesized from project-specific traces, summaries, and notes; should be human-reviewed before public release."
  },
  "labels": {
    "deduction_quality": 0.87,
    "novelty": 0.78,
    "plausibility": 0.78,
    "testability": 0.95,
    "implementation_difficulty": 0.68
  },
  "tags": [
    "failure",
    "circuit-lm"
  ]
}

Input:
{
  "task_type": "failed_invention_diagnosis",
  "domain": "MoE routing experiments",
  "problem": "Sparse routing experiments can collapse when routing dynamics over-commit or become spectrally unstable.",
  "objective": "Diagnose router collapse as a dynamics problem rather than only a load-balancing problem.",
  "constraints": [
    "measure expert utilization",
    "track spectral radius or routing dynamics proxy",
    "avoid one-expert dominance",
    "keep throughput acceptable"
  ],
  "prior_art_or_comparators": [
    "softmax routers",
    "load balancing loss",
    "spectral normalization",
    "router temperature schedules"
  ]
}

{
  "id": "zach_failed_invention_diagnosis_0004_srmoe-routing-collapse-and-spectral-instability",
  "title": "SRMoE Routing Collapse and Spectral Instability",
  "trace_type": "failed_invention_diagnosis",
  "domain": "MoE routing experiments",
  "problem": "Sparse routing experiments can collapse when routing dynamics over-commit or become spectrally unstable.",
  "objective": "Diagnose router collapse as a dynamics problem rather than only a load-balancing problem.",
  "constraints": [
    "measure expert utilization",
    "track spectral radius or routing dynamics proxy",
    "avoid one-expert dominance",
    "keep throughput acceptable"
  ],
  "prior_art": [
    "softmax routers",
    "load balancing loss",
    "spectral normalization",
    "router temperature schedules"
  ],
  "observations": [
    "Uploaded notes mention Star reasoning about SRMoE routing collapse, spectral radius and triadic architecture."
  ],
  "deductions": [
    "If routing probabilities sharpen too early, experts become early winners.",
    "If routing dynamics amplify perturbations, collapse is a stability failure.",
    "Therefore repair should control router commitment and spectral behavior."
  ],
  "abductive_leaps": [
    "Use spectral/dynamics constraints plus staged commitment."
  ],
  "candidate_mechanism": "Spectrally-Regularized Routing Curriculum",
  "mechanism_steps": [
    "monitor expert entropy",
    "add delayed router temperature anneal",
    "regularize routing Jacobian/spectral proxy",
    "rebalance only when specialization emerges",
    "evaluate collapse across seeds"
  ],
  "predicted_benefits": [
    "less expert collapse",
    "more stable specialization",
    "better diagnostic metrics"
  ],
  "failure_modes": [
    "regularizer hurts specialization",
    "spectral proxy expensive",
    "entropy looks balanced but experts duplicate"
  ],
  "minimal_test": "Train SRMoE variants with and without spectral regularization and delayed commitment; compare utilization and validation loss.",
  "evaluation_metrics": [
    "expert entropy",
    "collapse rate",
    "validation loss",
    "routing stability",
    "throughput"
  ],
  "source_basis": [
    "fine_tune_dataset.zip: 2026-04-09 notes mention SRMoE routing collapse and spectral radius."
  ],
  "provenance": {
    "origin": "synthesized_from_user_uploaded_archives_and_project_chat_history",
    "uploaded_files_used": [
      "TGChat export.zip",
      "fine_tune_dataset.zip"
    ],
    "redaction_applied": true,
    "raw_private_messages_included": false,
    "curation_note": "Gold-candidate record synthesized from project-specific traces, summaries, and notes; should be human-reviewed before public release."
  },
  "labels": {
    "deduction_quality": 0.84,
    "novelty": 0.7,
    "plausibility": 0.78,
    "testability": 0.88,
    "implementation_difficulty": 0.68
  },
  "tags": [
    "failure",
    "moe",
    "srmoe"
  ]
}

Input:
{
  "task_type": "failed_invention_diagnosis",
  "domain": "GPU inference operations",
  "problem": "A dual-5090 serving rig experienced BAR resize failure and Xid 79 GPU fallen-off-bus symptoms.",
  "objective": "Diagnose hardware instability as an inference reliability blocker, not merely a driver issue.",
  "constraints": [
    "must keep GPUs visible under load",
    "riser and PCIe link stability",
    "BIOS/BAR settings",
    "reproducible stress tests"
  ],
  "prior_art_or_comparators": [
    "PCIe ASPM tuning",
    "Resizable BAR",
    "riser replacement",
    "driver/kernel updates"
  ]
}

{
  "id": "zach_failed_invention_diagnosis_0005_rtx-5090-pcie-xid-79-bar-resize-failure",
  "title": "RTX 5090 PCIe Xid 79 / BAR Resize Failure",
  "trace_type": "failed_invention_diagnosis",
  "domain": "GPU inference operations",
  "problem": "A dual-5090 serving rig experienced BAR resize failure and Xid 79 GPU fallen-off-bus symptoms.",
  "objective": "Diagnose hardware instability as an inference reliability blocker, not merely a driver issue.",
  "constraints": [
    "must keep GPUs visible under load",
    "riser and PCIe link stability",
    "BIOS/BAR settings",
    "reproducible stress tests"
  ],
  "prior_art": [
    "PCIe ASPM tuning",
    "Resizable BAR",
    "riser replacement",
    "driver/kernel updates"
  ],
  "observations": [
    "Project memory records BAR resizing failed, Xid 79, riser lengths, kernel args and mixed PCIe links."
  ],
  "deductions": [
    "If a GPU falls off the bus, software optimization metrics are invalid.",
    "If link speed changes and BAR fails, physical/firmware PCIe instability is plausible.",
    "Therefore serving validation must include hardware health gates."
  ],
  "abductive_leaps": [
    "Put hardware gate checks before inference benchmarks."
  ],
  "candidate_mechanism": "Rig Health Gate for Inference Experiments",
  "mechanism_steps": [
    "record lspci link width/speed",
    "run nvidia-smi stress loop",
    "watch dmesg for Xid",
    "test risers individually",
    "gate benchmarks on clean health window"
  ],
  "predicted_benefits": [
    "prevents corrupted benchmark claims",
    "narrows failure to hardware/firmware",
    "improves rig reliability"
  ],
  "failure_modes": [
    "intermittent failures evade short tests",
    "BIOS flash risks board state"
  ],
  "minimal_test": "Run 30-minute GPU stress with telemetry capture before and after riser/kernel/BIOS changes; compare Xid and link stability.",
  "evaluation_metrics": [
    "Xid count",
    "link retrains",
    "GPU visibility",
    "throughput stability",
    "temperature/power"
  ],
  "source_basis": [
    "personal_context: hardware logs include BAR resize failed, Xid 79, PCIe/riser notes, kernel args."
  ],
  "provenance": {
    "origin": "synthesized_from_user_uploaded_archives_and_project_chat_history",
    "uploaded_files_used": [
      "TGChat export.zip",
      "fine_tune_dataset.zip"
    ],
    "redaction_applied": true,
    "raw_private_messages_included": false,
    "curation_note": "Gold-candidate record synthesized from project-specific traces, summaries, and notes; should be human-reviewed before public release."
  },
  "labels": {
    "deduction_quality": 0.83,
    "novelty": 0.48,
    "plausibility": 0.92,
    "testability": 0.93,
    "implementation_difficulty": 0.68
  },
  "tags": [
    "failure",
    "hardware",
    "gpu"
  ]
}

Input:
{
  "task_type": "failed_invention_diagnosis",
  "domain": "server operations",
  "problem": "The GPU rig had /dev/ipmi0 present but BMC unreachable, reducing remote recovery options.",
  "objective": "Diagnose why remote automation cannot assume out-of-band control.",
  "constraints": [
    "must verify BMC network path",
    "must not assume IPMI works from device node alone",
    "need local recovery plan",
    "avoid risky firmware steps without fallback"
  ],
  "prior_art_or_comparators": [
    "IPMI",
    "BMC reset",
    "remote hands",
    "BIOS recovery",
    "jumpbox workflows"
  ]
}

{
  "id": "zach_failed_invention_diagnosis_0006_bmc-ipmi-unreachable-recovery-failure",
  "title": "BMC/IPMI Unreachable Recovery Failure",
  "trace_type": "failed_invention_diagnosis",

    "safer rig management"

  "failure_modes": [
    "BMC network misconfigured",
    "credentials unavailable",
    "tool reports false readiness"

  "minimal_test": "Attempt local and network IPMI status checks; simulate recovery workflow before applying firmware changes.",

Input:
{
  "task_type": "failed_invention_diagnosis",
  "domain": "dataset curation",
  "problem": "Uploading full raw chat/fine-tune data risks leaking personal identifiers, unreleased IP and low-value generic content.",
  "objective": "Diagnose why more data is not automatically better for an invention model.",
  "constraints": [
    "private dataset target",
    "strip phone/PII",
    "dedupe",
    "exclude generic fineweb/cache data",
    "separate raw from distilled traces"
  ],
  "prior_art_or_comparators": [
    "instruction tuning",
    "conversation logs",
    "data filtering",
    "PII redaction"
  ]
}

{
  "id": "zach_failed_invention_diagnosis_0007_raw-personal-dataset-leakage-ip-dilution",
  "title": "Raw Personal Dataset Leakage / IP Dilution",
  "trace_type": "failed_invention_diagnosis",
  "domain": "dataset curation",
  "problem": "Uploading full raw chat/fine-tune data risks leaking personal identifiers, unreleased IP and low-value generic content.",
  "objective": "Diagnose why more data is not automatically better for an invention model.",
  "constraints": [
    "private dataset target",
    "strip phone/PII",
    "dedupe",
    "exclude generic fineweb/cache data",
    "separate raw from distilled traces"
  ],
  "prior_art": [
    "instruction tuning",
    "conversation logs",
    "data filtering",
    "PII redaction"
  ],
  "observations": [
    "TGChat export includes assistant warning that raw data contains research process and identifying details; fine_tune zip includes large generic/cache files."
  ],
  "deductions": [
    "If raw logs include private identifiers, private HF is safer but still not clean training data.",
    "If generic web/cache dominates, it dilutes invention traces.",
    "Therefore gold traces should be distilled and redacted from raw source."
  ],
  "abductive_leaps": [
    "Use distilled trace records instead of raw chat dumps for the invention dataset."
  ],
  "candidate_mechanism": "Private Redacted Trace Distillation",
  "mechanism_steps": [
    "scan source files",
    "exclude generic caches",
    "dedupe near duplicates",
    "summarize evidence instead of copying raw messages",
    "emit trace records with provenance and redaction flag"
  ],
  "predicted_benefits": [
    "higher signal",
    "lower privacy risk",
    "cleaner fine-tuning objective"
  ],
  "failure_modes": [
    "over-redaction removes useful details",
    "synthetic summaries drift from source"
  ],
  "minimal_test": "Compare model trained on raw chat slice vs distilled trace slice on invention-eval prompts and privacy leakage probes.",
  "evaluation_metrics": [
    "eval score",
    "PII leakage rate",
    "duplicate rate",
    "format validity",
    "novelty/plausibility score"
  ],
  "source_basis": [
    "TGChat export/fine_tune_dataset.zip: raw dataset/privacy discussions; current curation constraints."
  ],
  "provenance": {
    "origin": "synthesized_from_user_uploaded_archives_and_project_chat_history",
